Andhra Pradesh Districts Formation

# Andhra Pradesh Districts Formation

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Nov 01, 2024

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!YZqL!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F92ba14dc-8b6a-47a2-823b-c5b7a1c1a563_589x583.png)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!YZqL!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F92ba14dc-8b6a-47a2-823b-c5b7a1c1a563_589x583.png)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

On the occasion of Andhra Pradesh formation day, taking a look here at the formation of each district and their significance.

Nellore, historically known as Vikrama Simhapuri, stands as one of Andhra Pradesh’s oldest districts, its roots stretching deep into the folds of time. Once part of the legendary Dandakaranya forests, it witnessed the rise and fall of empires—from the Mauryas and Satavahanas to the Pallavas, Cholas, Kakatiyas, and Vijayanagara rulers.

In the 13th century, it flourished as a cultural beacon when *Tikkana Somayaji*, the revered Telugu poet and minister, translated the Mahabharata into Telugu under the patronage of the Nellore Cholas.

Later, the region came under the sway of the *Nawabs of Arcot*, with *Azim-Ud-Daula* ceding control to the British in 1781. Nellore was formally established as a district in *1792*, becoming a key administrative and cultural node in the Madras Presidency.

Today, Nellore is celebrated for its lush *rice fields*, serene *beaches* like *Mypadu*, and sacred sites such as the *Sriranganatha Swamy Temple*, a spiritual anchor on the banks of the Penna River. It also hosts *Sriharikota*, India’s premier space launch center, and shares the biodiverse *Pulicat Lake* with neighboring Tamil Nadu—a haven for migratory birds and ecological wonder.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!0krc!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd987d23b-4a05-46ab-a064-1bfd44f94721_630x680.jpeg)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!0krc!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd987d23b-4a05-46ab-a064-1bfd44f94721_630x680.jpeg "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

In the wake of the Treaty of Srirangapatnam (1792), the British East India Company gained strategic control over parts of the Deccan. By 1800, Nizam Ali Khan, the second Asaf Jahi ruler, formally ceded the territories of Kadapa, Kurnool, Bellary, and Anantapur to the British. This marked a turning point in Rayalaseema’s administrative history.

By 1808, the British reorganized these lands, forming the Kadapa district with Siddavatam—a fort town on the Penna River—as its initial headquarters. However, due to frequent flooding and logistical challenges, the seat was shifted to Kadapa town in 1812, where it remains today.

Kadapa, once known as Cuddapah, is steeped in mythic and historical resonance. It is believed to be part of the ancient Dandakaranya forest, through which Rama and Sita wandered during their exile. The region flourished under various dynasties—from the Mauryas and Satavahanas to the Cholas, Vijayanagara, and Nawabs of Kurnool—each leaving behind forts, temples, and poetic legacies.

Kadapa gets it's name from Devuni Gadapa(Threshold of God), referring to the Venkateswara Temple here, that was a starting point to many pilgrims going to Tirumala. Also famous for the Gandikota fort and Canyon, Pushpagiri Temple complex, Vontimitta Ramalayam and Brahmamgari Matam.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!2953!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fefe5ff55-5252-460e-b889-4429a02ca0a1_800x574.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!2953!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fefe5ff55-5252-460e-b889-4429a02ca0a1_800x574.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!gQt5!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fc1e196a2-c207-43d0-a37b-66e3c12940f2_800x600.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![Pylon commemorative of 200 years](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!a_w5!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F3a82c695-e8ab-4a18-8deb-f8ce282aa52a_250x282.jpeg "Pylon commemorative of 200 years")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!AeSe!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F95741760-03f8-4932-82b6-db50a0126c4c_500x400.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!AeSe!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F95741760-03f8-4932-82b6-db50a0126c4c_500x400.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Founded in 1808 under British administration, Kurnool district originally encompassed the vast region of Bellary, forming a strategic gateway to the Deccan. This reorganization followed the Treaty of Srirangapatnam (1792) and the Nizam’s cession of territory in 1800, which handed over large swathes of Rayalaseema to the British.

Kurnool’s early history is shaped by its Nawabi legacy, with rulers like Aluf Khan and Rasool Khan establishing a martial and architectural imprint—most notably the Konda Reddy Fort. The district’s administrative and cultural significance grew steadily under colonial rule.

A major turning point came with the Andhra State Act of 1953, which carved out a new Telugu-speaking state from the Madras Presidency. This act act also redrew linguistic boundaries, Bellary’s Kannada-speaking areas were transferred to Mysore State (now Karnataka), while Telugu-speaking areas remained with Kurnool district and some regions were reassigned to Anantapur district for administrative coherence

Kurnool briefly served as the capital of Andhra State from 1953 to 1956, before the formation of Andhra Pradesh with Hyderabad as its capital. This moment marked Kurnool as a symbol of linguistic pride and transitional governance.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Yi8T!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F34a49d7d-ceb4-46cc-a1d9-690bc7f214e9_640x480.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Yi8T!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F34a49d7d-ceb4-46cc-a1d9-690bc7f214e9_640x480.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

**After the linguistic reorganization of states in 1953, Telugu-speaking towns like Yemmiganur, Alur, and Adoni were retained in Kurnool district, while Kanekallu and Bommanahallu were reassigned to Anantapur district.**

One of the Dwadasa Jyotirlingams Srisailam is located here, as well as the Belum caves, Ahobilam Narasimha Kshetra, Mantralayam, Yaganti and Mahanandi.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!OvcT!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fa566c4d8-4e48-4272-b75c-29e94a81e3f3_680x300.png)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!j-VP!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd7d55e01-61d3-45f7-a558-94447afc4cf8_360x243.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!8Eek!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F1e537399-bb01-4395-afb6-e347c5e56162_360x200.png "Image")

![Ahobilam Nava Narasimha and Other Temples - A Complete Guide](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ieqO!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F6bb7ffca-581f-4546-9dcf-e91faed74765_1600x1200.jpeg "Ahobilam Nava Narasimha and Other Temples - A Complete Guide")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Cradled by the sacred waters of the Krishna River, this district—once known as Machilipatnam district—was formally constituted in 1859 under British rule. Its name honors the mighty river that nourishes its lands and civilizations, flowing from the Western Ghats to the Bay of Bengal, where it meets the sea near Hamsaladeevi.

At its heart lies Vijayawada, Andhra Pradesh’s second-largest city, a bustling confluence of commerce, culture, and connectivity. The city’s railway junction is among the largest in India, a symbol of its strategic importance since colonial times. Towering above the city on Indrakeeladri Hill is the revered **Kanaka Durga Temple**, where the goddess is believed to have manifested to vanquish evil and bless the land.

To the east lies Machilipatnam, once a flourishing port under the Golconda Sultanate and later a key node in European maritime trade. It became one of the earliest municipalities in India, bearing witness to centuries of global exchange, colonial ambition, and local resilience.

Kuchipudi classical dance originated here, and the district is an economic, cultural, literary hub.Designer of our national flag Pingali Venkayya is from Machilipatnam, as are many movie stars, media magnates, writers, freedom fighters, businessmen.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Cs0a!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Faee08c1b-14d0-4bf0-979b-1cc2ee2c9331_800x533.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Cs0a!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Faee08c1b-14d0-4bf0-979b-1cc2ee2c9331_800x533.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!TnUw!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fecefd888-05b2-46ab-96a1-42021ad5dcc5_800x533.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!TnUw!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fecefd888-05b2-46ab-96a1-42021ad5dcc5_800x533.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Formed in 1882 from the larger Bellary district under British administration, Anantapur derives its name from Anantasagaramu—a vast tank built during the Vijayanagara Empire. Some historians attribute its construction to Anantarasa Chikkavodeya, a minister of Bukka I, while others suggest it was named after Bukka’s queen. Either way, the name evokes the idea of an “endless ocean,” symbolizing abundance in a drought-prone land.

Anantapur’s geography is marked by arid plains and resilient communities. It is bounded by Kurnool to the north, Chittoor to the southeast, YSR Kadapa district to the east, and Karnataka to the west.

The district is famous for the Lepakshi temple, Puttaparthy Sai Baba Ashram, Penugonda, Gooty forts, Kadiri Narasimha Temple, Tadipatri Bugga Ramalingeswara Temple and Guntakal Railway Junction.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!aRGg!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F653e9cf9-4874-4015-9300-5ffbf5073fce_680x453.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!aRGg!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F653e9cf9-4874-4015-9300-5ffbf5073fce_680x453.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!UeIf!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F22f5df87-d40a-4dcc-8be5-339572020bc1_828x650.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!UeIf!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F22f5df87-d40a-4dcc-8be5-339572020bc1_828x650.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Guntur, carved out as a separate district on *October 1, 1904*, emerged from the reorganization of *Krishna and Nellore districts* under British rule. Its formation included taluks like *Tenali, Bapatla, Guntur, Sattenapalli, Narasaraopet, Vinukonda,* and *Palnadu* from Krishna, and *Ongole* from Nellore.

It is famous for it’s chillies, spicy food, hot weather, was formed from Krishna district in 1904. It was the hub of the freedom struggle in AP, famous for the Palnadu Satyagraha, Chirala Satyagraha.

Some of the great freedom fighters of Andhra Pradesh like Kanneganti Hanumanthu, Duggirala Gopalakrishnayya, Acharya N.G.Ranga were from Guntur, the district is also a commercial, educational hub, and many Telugu cinema artists, directors come from there. The district is also famous for Amaravati temple, Vundavalli caves, Kondavidu fort, Narasimha Temple at Mangalagiri.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!g9du!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F6a618ac6-0269-4f06-9b26-9858fe4d7d07_680x680.jpeg)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!g9du!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F6a618ac6-0269-4f06-9b26-9858fe4d7d07_680x680.jpeg "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ABEV!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4251bc82-6871-4918-aa99-e008bdfec9fc_800x600.jpeg)

![Mangalagiri](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ABEV!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4251bc82-6871-4918-aa99-e008bdfec9fc_800x600.jpeg "Mangalagiri")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Formed in 1911, Chittoor district emerged from various regions, **Chittoor, Palamaneru, Tirupati** from the North Arcot district of the Madras Presidency, **Madanapalle and Vayalpadu** from Kadapa district and the **Zamindari estates** of Pileru, Sri Kalahasti, Punganur, and Karvetinagar.

A major shift occurred in *1960* following the *Pataskar Award*, part of India’s linguistic state reorganization, when several *Tamil-speaking villages* in Chittoor were merged with *Chingleput* and *Thiruvallur* districts of *Tamil Nadu, while the Telugu speaking areas of Satyavedu, Kuppam* were transferred from *Chingleput* to *Chittoor.*

The district is known for the Shri Venkateswara Temple at Tirumala, Srikalahasti one of the Panchabhoota Kshetrams, Horsley Hills, Kanipakam Vinayaka, Talakona forests, Nagulapuram temple and Chandragiri, erstwhile capital of Vijayanagara Empire.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!lSKa!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F2843e9f8-4abd-42ba-bda7-6aea36301342_660x376.png)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!vQbu!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fab660a1b-425f-43ba-a4eb-5fdfa8ca30c6_800x533.jpeg)

![Galigopuram of Vinayaka Temple, Kanipakam](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!vQbu!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fab660a1b-425f-43ba-a4eb-5fdfa8ca30c6_800x533.jpeg "Galigopuram of Vinayaka Temple, Kanipakam")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

In 1823, under British rule, the expansive Rajahmundry district was created—encompassing the fertile deltas of the Godavari and Krishna rivers. This administrative unit was vast, culturally rich, and agriculturally vital.

By 1859, the British reorganized the territory as **Krishna district** was carved out as a separate entity and the remaining region became **Godavari district**, with Rajahmundry as its headquarters

In 1925, Godavari district was split into, **East Godavari**, with **Kakinada** as its headquarters—emerging as a coastal hub of trade, education, and culture

**West Godavari**, with **Eluru** as its headquarters—known for its agrarian strength, canal networks, and spiritual heritage

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!KOS1!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8651d036-5fc4-447e-adf5-6a868a541b9b_680x510.jpeg)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!KOS1!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8651d036-5fc4-447e-adf5-6a868a541b9b_680x510.jpeg "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!CHwF!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8834f387-36cb-4db2-90d0-35c83a5be176_672x504.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!CHwF!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8834f387-36cb-4db2-90d0-35c83a5be176_672x504.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

This division mirrored the bifurcation of the river itself, which splits into two distributaries near Dowleswaram, flowing toward the Bay of Bengal. The Godavari districts became synonymous with, **rice cultivation**, earning the title “Rice Bowl of Andhra”, **cultural renaissance**, with poets, reformers, and freedom fighters emerging from its soil and **holy places.**, from Draksharamam and Kotipalli to Dwaraka Tirumala and Perupalem.

In 1802, the British carved out Visakhapatnam as a revenue district, drawing from the ancient territories of the Kalinga region. This new district included Srikakulam, which was then part of the larger Ganjam district under the Madras Presidency. The region’s strategic coastline, mineral wealth, and tribal heartlands made it a vital node in the colonial administrative grid.

A major administrative shift came in 1936, when the British created the separate Odisha Province to consolidate Odia-speaking regions. As part of this reorganization, **Rayagada**, **Jeypore**, **Koraput**, and the **entire Ganjam district** were transferred to Odisha, while **Srikakulam**, despite its historical ties to Ganjam, remained with Andhra, preserving its Telugu identity

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!85-_!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Feca86610-3804-4131-bb1e-6662d33b3b1b_680x451.jpeg)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!85-_!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Feca86610-3804-4131-bb1e-6662d33b3b1b_680x451.jpeg "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Vizag still has a strong Odiya connect, the Science Department in AU University was funded by the Maharajah of Jeypore. Daspalla Hills, gets it's name from the erstwhile princely state in Odisha, whose land it was.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!0OkI!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F9c9c2388-25bc-4f76-b594-0377bf2fec99_680x451.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!0OkI!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F9c9c2388-25bc-4f76-b594-0377bf2fec99_680x451.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Formed in 1950 from the northern reaches of Visakhapatnam district, Srikakulam stands as Andhra Pradesh’s northernmost district, bordering Odisha. Its terrain—crisscrossed by the Nagavali and Vamsadhara rivers—was once part of the ancient Kalinga kingdom, whose influence stretched from Cuttack to Pithapuram.

This region has witnessed the rise and fall of dynasties, Satavahanas, Vasishtiputas, Eastern Gangas, and Gajapatis ruled here for centuries. and later, it came under Kutubshahi and Nizam rule, and was briefly assigned to French India in 1753.

It is famous for Arasavalli, one of the few Sun temples in India, as well as Sri Kurmam, where Vishnu is worshipped as Kurmavatara and Srimukhalingam temple complex.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!gEoG!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F1e5a1791-ac7e-44f5-b28b-821051e90ac2_1200x770.jpeg)

![Arasavalli temple wears a festive look - The Hindu](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!gEoG!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F1e5a1791-ac7e-44f5-b28b-821051e90ac2_1200x770.jpeg "Arasavalli temple wears a festive look - The Hindu")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!2PlV!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F722e25d5-169c-4598-99c8-e3fd9b5acc37_800x600.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!2PlV!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F722e25d5-169c-4598-99c8-e3fd9b5acc37_800x600.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!bCEI!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fa94de113-454a-4ae2-b609-3b60032d8b36_800x600.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!bCEI!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fa94de113-454a-4ae2-b609-3b60032d8b36_800x600.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Established as a separate district on *June 1, 1979*, *Vizianagaram* emerged from the cultural and historical heartlands of *Srikakulam* and *Visakhapatnam*. Its formation marked the recognition of a region steeped in martial valor, educational excellence, and spiritual antiquity.

It is renowned for the historic Battle of Bobbili, the educational legacy of Vizianagaram town, and the ancient Rama temple at Ramatheertham.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!TFmy!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F038bf0c3-fc6e-46af-b419-245964af5ebc_1000x751.jpeg)

![Bobbili Fort Tourism (Vizianagaram) (2024 - 2025) - A Complete Travel Guide](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!TFmy!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F038bf0c3-fc6e-46af-b419-245964af5ebc_1000x751.jpeg "Bobbili Fort Tourism (Vizianagaram) (2024 - 2025) - A Complete Travel Guide")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!avtV!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fb4ce355a-009a-4395-8c88-6d9463af8572_800x600.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!avtV!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fb4ce355a-009a-4395-8c88-6d9463af8572_800x600.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

On *February 2, 1970*, a new district was born—*Prakasam*—carved from the heartlands of three older districts, *Addanki*, *Chirala*, and *Ongole from Guntur,* *Kandukur*, *Kanigiri*, and *Darsi from Nellore and* *Markapur* , *Giddaluru from Kurnool.*

This administrative reorganization brought together coastal plains, forested hills, and agrarian heartlands into a single, cohesive district. The headquarters was established at **Ongole**, a town known for its ancient Buddhist roots and the globally renowned *Ongole bull*.

The district was named after **Tanguturi Prakasam Pantulu**, affectionately called *Andhra Kesari* (Lion of Andhra). A fearless barrister, editor of *Swarajya*, and a Gandhian stalwart, Prakasam led the *Simon Go Back* protests in Madras and later became the *first Chief Minister of Andhra State* in 1953. His legacy of courage, integrity, and service continues to inspire generations.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!6k5w!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fceb27314-cfb5-4e02-be30-d592fe8c1f67_276x360.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!6k5w!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fceb27314-cfb5-4e02-be30-d592fe8c1f67_276x360.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

The district is famous for the Ongole Gitta, one of the finest bull breeds, temples at Tripurantakam, Bhairavakona, beaches at Vodarevu.

The Agency area, covering Vizag, Vizianagaram, Srikakulam, Godavari districts, known for it’s thick forests, valleys, flanking the Eastern Ghats, was formed as a separate district in 2022 named after the legendary revolutionary Alluri Sitarama Raju, and has the beautiful Araku Valley, Borra Caves and Anantagiri plantations from where you get the Araku Coffee.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!yUlk!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ffea840e7-1ea9-43c3-ac79-4e77d1efc55f_800x600.jpeg)

![undefined](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!yUlk!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ffea840e7-1ea9-43c3-ac79-4e77d1efc55f_800x600.jpeg "undefined")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)
